message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracker — PinkSpire tx-engine progress on ft-3681t.4.3

Update .beads/issues.jsonl with PinkSpire's implementation comment on
ft-3681t.4.3 documenting tx-engine commit/compensation phase work:
lifecycle state validation, deterministic per-step receipts, failure
boundaries, KillSwitchBlocked/PauseSuspended outcomes, reverse-order
compensation, and updated E2E assertions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2781,7 +2781,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-3681t.4.1.2","title":"[robot][compile] Resolve remaining Commands::Status initializer drift and stabilize robot alias tests","description":"Follow-on slice for ft-3681t.4.1: fix any remaining Commands::Status initializer shape drift in parser/alias tests after robot alias expansion, keep NTM-compat robot command surface tests green, and document validation evidence. Includes targeted test updates in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs and rch-offloaded validation runs.","notes":"2026-03-04 CoralCastle: implemented robot alias-surface stabilization in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs by expanding quick-start alias guidance and adding parser tests for additional aliases (panes, tail, write, watch, grep, eventlog). Validation: rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs PASS. rch exec -- env CARGO_HOME=/tmp/ft-coralcastle-cargo-home CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-coralcastle-target-robot cargo test -p frankenterm alias_ -- --nocapture PASS (11 alias tests in src/main.rs). Workspace-wide rch check/clippy/fmt attempts were initiated; remote worker connectivity intermittently failed (fail-open local) and workspace-wide runs remain noisy/expensive under concurrent swarm compile load with known unrelated formatting/compile debt in other files.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralCastle","created_at":"2026-03-04T01:11:09.180054Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-04T01:32:17.641546Z","closed_at":"2026-03-04T01:32:17.641520Z","close_reason":"Implemented alias-surface stabilization in main.rs and validated targeted alias tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","ntm-convergence","robot-api"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.1.2","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.4.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-04T01:11:09.180054Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.4.2","title":"[robot] Streaming event/wait interfaces for deterministic automation","description":"Implement streaming event subscriptions and wait primitives with filtering, correlation IDs, cursor checkpoints, and bounded replay so autonomous agents coordinate against real system state with deterministic ordering/reconnect semantics rather than polling heuristics.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Event streams support filtering, cursoring, checkpoint resume, and bounded replay.
2. Wait primitives are race-safe, timeout-safe, and cancellation-safe.
3. Ordering, dedupe, and reconnect/restart semantics are deterministic for automation clients.
4. Backpressure/flow-control behavior prevents silent event loss or unbounded memory growth under high throughput.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleOwl","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:12:42.884620Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-04T03:16:09.288006Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["automation","events","robot-api","streaming"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:16:58.253203Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:12:42.884620Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.2","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:16:57.726625Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1853,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.2","author":"GentleOwl","text":"Progress update: implemented cursor/replay event-stream slice. Changes: (1) added StorageHandle::get_events_stream(EventStreamQuery) with deterministic id-ordered resume query in crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs; (2) added unit test storage_handle_event_stream_cursor_resume; (3) added robot events CLI args --cursor/--replay-limit and response fields cursor/next_cursor/replay_limit/order in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs; (4) wired cursor-mode event query path and next_cursor checkpoint emission. Validation attempts (via rch): cargo fmt --check, cargo check/clippy/tests. Current blocker: workspace currently fails cargo check on pre-existing unrelated errors in crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_sdk.rs (missing new fields in ConnectorManifest/ConnectorRegistryConfig initializers). Also workspace has unrelated warnings in ftui-text/connector_sdk. I fixed one ownership bug in my changes (next_cursor computed before consuming events).","created_at":"2026-03-04T03:16:09Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.4.2.1","title":"[ft-3681t.4.2][support] Preserve robot-events cursor checkpoint across empty replay pages","description":"Cursor-mode `ft robot events` currently returns `next_cursor = null` when a replay page is empty, which weakens checkpoint-resume semantics compared with the audit stream path. Update the response contract so empty cursor pages preserve the caller checkpoint, and add focused regressions for empty and non-empty cursor pages plus argument/error behavior.","status":"blocked","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PearlBarn","created_at":"2026-03-07T06:07:50.014034Z","created_by":"PearlBarn","updated_at":"2026-03-07T06:44:49.456543Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contracts","events","robot-api","streaming"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.2.1","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.4.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T06:07:50.014034Z","created_by":"PearlBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1926,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.2.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented the robot-events cursor checkpoint fix in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs and added focused helper regressions. Verification is currently blocked by unrelated dirty-worktree compile failures in crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs, crates/frankenterm-core/src/fleet_launcher.rs, and crates/frankenterm-core/src/swarm_pipeline.rs during rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-pearlbarn-target cargo test -p frankenterm robot_events_ -- --nocapture.","created_at":"2026-03-07T06:23:52Z"},{"id":1930,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.2.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Patch-specific tests are green in the warmed isolated target: robot_events_next_cursor_preserves_checkpoint_for_empty_replay_page, robot_events_next_cursor_advances_to_last_event_id, robot_events_cursor_validation_rejects_negative_values, and robot_events_replay_limit_validation_rejects_zero all passed. A broader robot_events_ filter hit an unrelated pre-existing stack overflow in tests::cli_robot_events_dry_run_flag. Workspace cargo check advanced deep into the graph but failed on environment capacity: /tmp/ft-pearlbarn-target ran out of disk space while compiling frankenterm-core.","created_at":"2026-03-07T06:43:49Z"}]}

High-impact commands support dry-run and approval gates. 2. Execution receipts include enough detail for audit and debugging. 3. Rollback/compensation behavior is explicit.

Q5. Compensation now validates lifecycle state, runs in reverse commit order, emits receipts, and reports compensated/failed/skipped counts. Updated tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_interfaces.sh assertions for explicit commit/rollback counts/receipts and added paused + safe-mode scenarios. Verification via rch with isolated caches: cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib tx_ -- --nocapture passed (145 passed, includes new plan.rs tests); cargo test -p frankenterm mission_robot_tx_helpers_build_expected_inputs_and_reports -- --nocapture passed. Deterministic e2e script currently fails before exercising tx logic because remote rch cargo run cannot resolve sibling path dependency /home/ubuntu/rch/frankenterm/frankenredis/crates/fr-config on the worker; artifacts in tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_interfaces_20260307_185929.*.","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:05:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.4.4","title":"[robot] Machine contracts, SDK generation, ntm-compat shim, replay tests","description":"Publish durable machine contracts (schemas/specs/examples), generate client SDK surfaces, add an ntm compatibility shim for migration acceleration, and enforce behavior via replay-based contract tests.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Contracts and generated clients stay in lockstep with implementation. 2. Compatibility shim supports migration-critical workflows. 3. Replay tests catch protocol regressions.

Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5.  {"id":"ft-3681t.4.5","title":"[quality] Robot API contract/replay and compatibility test matrix","description":"Build a contract matrix that validates schema stability, deterministic semantics, replay correctness, and ntm-compat migration guarantees for machine interfaces.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Contract tests cover every robot command/event/error surface. 2. Replay suite verifies determinism and backward-compat migration expectations. 3. Compatibility failures are pinpointed with actionable diffs. 4. Includes unit/integration/e2e contract tests with detailed structured logs/artifacts.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.

Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.

Failure/retry semantics prevent unintended repeated side effects. Q1.
